macro_segments.title: Who may shave and launder during Chol HaMoed: The Mishnah's categories and exceptions

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the new perek and overview of Chol HaMoed practices
Those permitted to shave: overseas travelers, freed prisoners and captives, released from nidui, released from vows
Those permitted to launder clothes and towels: same categories plus special items
General rule: all others are forbidden to shave or launder on Chol HaMoed

macro_segments.title: The reason for the prohibition: forcing preparation beforehand to avoid appearing disheveled on Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's question: Why are others forbidden if not a standard melacha?
Rabbi Elazar's answer: to prevent entering the holiday looking disheveled and unpresentable
Discussion of whether Chol HaMoed prohibitions are based on the 39 melachot or on other principles

macro_segments.title: Non-evident circumstances and the problem of allowing exceptions without clear categories

micro_segments.title: Rav Zera's question: Can someone lost in a lost object on erev haregel shave during Chol HaMoed?
Abaye's answer: We don't make distinctions in cases not self-evident (lo plug)
The case of someone with only one shirt: exception despite non-evident circumstances
The rule refined: evidence makes an exception permissible even if circumstances are special

macro_segments.title: Debate on overseas travel: Rabbi Yehuda vs. the Sages on discretionary vs. necessary journeys

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda forbids shaving for those returning from overseas (without permission); Sages permit it
Rava's refinement: distinction between vacation travel and travel for livelihood or profit
The Gemara clarifies the debate applies only to profit-motivated travel, not extremes

macro_segments.title: Special cases: minors born during Chol HaMoed and infants permitted to receive haircuts

micro_segments.title: Shmuel: a minor born during Chol HaMoed is permitted to have his hair cut
Debate on whether birth before Chol HaMoed changes the ruling; Rav Ashi's clarification
Inference about minors and mourning: permitted to receive haircuts during aveilus since not obligated

macro_segments.title: The relationship between Chol HaMoed practices and mourning (aveilus) practices

micro_segments.title: Gemara principle: a mourner does not practice aveilus on Yom Tov due to communal rejoicing
Question of whether aveilus is biblical or rabbinic; Tosafot's analysis
Does nidui (excommunication) apply on Yom Tov? Connection to court proceedings on Chol HaMoed

macro_segments.title: Evidence that nidui applies on Yom Tov based on the need for compliance in court cases

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's proof: court cases proceed on Chol HaMoed, requiring nidui as enforcement tool
Abaye's objection: courts may only investigate cases, not give final ruling, on Chol HaMoed
Problem with postponement: executing death penalty requires immediate ruling; procedure to handle this
Conclusion: even with the timing solution, nidui likely applies on Yom Tov, unlike aveilus

topical_tags.term: Shaving and haircuts on Chol HaMoed
Rabbi Yehuda vs. the Sages on overseas travel
